summary.en: You deliver the value entirely manually to a tiny set of customers. Maximum learning, minimum infrastructure. Validates desirability + viability + the unit-of-value before any product exists.
summary.zh: 对一小群客户完全手工交付价值。学习最多、基础设施最少。在产品都还不存在的时候,验证需求性 + 可营性 + 价值单元。

sources.label: Bland & Osterwalder · Testing Business Ideas · Wiley · 2019 · Validation / 4-10 Concierge
Eric Ries · The Lean Startup · Crown Business · 2011

headline.en: Manual PDFs before building automated seller tools.
headline.zh: 先人工交付 PDF，再决定是否自动化卖房工具。

story.en: realtor.com heard that people selling a home often struggled to coordinate selling and buying across two markets. Instead of building a full tool, the team offered a call-to-action, asked qualifying questions, and had product manager Dave Masters manually assemble personalized PDF insights from existing realtor.com data for each signup.
story.zh: realtor.com 发现卖房用户常常苦于同时处理卖房和买房、还涉及两个不同市场。团队没有先做完整工具，而是放出一个 CTA，收集一组问题，然后由产品经理 Dave Masters 手工把 realtor.com 现有数据拼成个性化 PDF 发给每个报名用户。

hypothesis.en: The team believed that sellers on realtor.com who planned to sell within a year were also likely to buy at the same time and would value side-by-side market timing insights.
hypothesis.zh: 团队相信：realtor.com 上计划一年内卖房的用户，很可能也会同时买房，并且会重视两个市场并排对比的时机洞察。

experiment.en: A concierge workflow delivered the promised insight manually. Users clicked a CTA, completed questions, and received a custom PDF created by the product manager from existing site data, plus a meeting link for follow-up learning.
experiment.zh: 团队用 Concierge 工作流人工交付承诺价值。用户点击 CTA、回答问题后，产品经理用站内现有数据手工制作定制 PDF，并在邮件中附上会议链接，用来继续学习。

evidence.en: The test generated more than 80 signups in a few minutes, far above the team's expectation of about 30 signups in three hours.
evidence.zh: 实验几分钟内获得 80 多个报名，远高于团队原本预估的 3 小时约 30 个报名。

insights.en: The audience and problem were real, but the team also learned the operational burden of high-volume concierge delivery and the need to reserve time to fulfil the promise.
insights.zh: 目标人群和问题都是真实的；同时团队也学到，高量级 Concierge 会带来明显人工交付压力，必须预留履约时间，不能只看报名数字。

actions.en: The team persevered with in-app experiments and tested seller-specific features, including a feature stub for a 'Selling-Tools' tab.
actions.zh: 团队继续推进应用内实验，并测试卖房用户专属功能，包括一个指向「Selling-Tools」标签页的功能桩。

examples.source: Bland & Osterwalder · Testing Business Ideas · Wiley · 2019 · realtor.com case study, pp. 252-253

text.en:
Replace this with one fragile service-delivery assumption.

e.g. [Target customers] want [outcome] badly enough to accept a high-touch manual service before software exists.
text.zh:
替换成一条最脆弱的人工交付假设。

例如：[目标客户] 对 [结果] 的需求强到愿意在软件还不存在时接受高接触度人工服务。

text.en:
We believe [target customers] will use and value a manually delivered version of [offering].

To verify, we will serve 5 pilot customers for 2 weeks using people, spreadsheets, and manual workflows.

And measure completed jobs, repeat requests, willingness-to-pay language, delivery effort, and satisfaction.

We are right if ≥ 4/5 complete the target job and ≥ 3 ask to continue or pay.
text.zh:
我们相信 [目标客户] 会使用并重视一个人工交付版的 [提供物]。

为了验证，我们将 用人、表格和人工流程为 5 位试点客户服务 2 周。

并测量 完成任务数、重复请求、付费意愿语言、交付人力和满意度。

如果 ≥ 4/5 完成目标任务且 ≥ 3 人要求继续或付费 = 通过。

text.en:
Recruit: 5 high-pain customers from interviews or waitlist; require a real current problem and agreement to weekly check-ins. Charge a small pilot fee if viability matters.

Deliver: promise one narrow outcome, then fulfil it manually with a shared inbox, spreadsheet, and daily operator checklist. Cap scope: max 3 requests/customer/week or one core workflow per customer.

Record: log every request, turnaround time, rework, customer comment, and operator minute. Hold a 20-minute debrief with each customer at the end of week 2.
text.zh:
招募：从访谈或候补名单中找 5 位高痛客户；必须有真实当前问题，并同意每周回访。若要测可营性，收一笔小额试点费。

交付：只承诺一个窄结果，然后用共享邮箱、表格和每日运营清单人工完成。限制范围：每客户每周最多 3 个请求，或只跑一个核心流程。

记录：记录每个请求、周转时间、返工、客户评论和运营分钟数。第 2 周结束时对每位客户做 20 分钟复盘访谈。

text.en:
Pass: ≥ 4/5 customers reach the promised outcome; ≥ 3 request continuation or agree to pay; average delivery effort ≤ [max operator minutes] per job.

Fail: customers do not send real requests, abandon before completion, or value requires unsustainably custom work.

Inconclusive: customers love the outcome but labour is too high; identify one repeatable step for Wizard of Oz automation testing.
text.zh:
通过：≥ 4/5 客户达到承诺结果；≥ 3 人要求继续或同意付费；平均交付人力 ≤ 每任务 [最大运营分钟数]。

失败：客户没有真实请求、中途放弃，或价值依赖不可规模化的高度定制。

不清晰：客户喜欢结果但人工成本太高；找出一个可重复步骤，用 Wizard of Oz 测自动化价值。

text.en:
Fill in after running.

Pilot customers: __ ; Completed target jobs: __ ; Continuation / pay asks: __ ; Avg operator minutes/job: __ .

Most repeated manual step: __ . Strongest quote: __ . Verdict: validated / invalidated / inconclusive.
text.zh:
实验后填写。

试点客户：__；完成目标任务：__；要求继续 / 付费人数：__；平均每任务运营分钟：__。

最高频人工步骤：__。最强原话：__。结论：已验证 / 已证伪 / 不清晰。

text.en:
Pick one based on the result.

• Persevere → turn the repeated service steps into a Wizard of Oz or early workflow product.
• Pivot → narrow the customer segment or outcome to the part customers value most.
• Kill → if customers will not repeatedly request the service, stop building the product wrapper.
text.zh:
根据结果选一个。

• Persevere → 把重复服务步骤转成 Wizard of Oz 或早期流程产品。
• Pivot → 缩小客群或结果范围，只保留客户最看重的部分。
• Kill → 如果客户不会重复提出服务请求，不要继续做产品外壳。
